pattern: Capping every value at a threshold makes the resulting sum monotonic in the threshold, so the best cap can be pinned down by searching that range and breaking ties toward the smaller value.
hints: As the chosen cap rises, the mutated sum never decreases - it climbs monotonically until it flattens at the untouched total.
Because the sum is monotonic in the cap, you can binary-search the cap over the range from zero to the array's maximum.
Find where the mutated sum crosses the target, then compare the cap just below and just above it; pick whichever lands closer, and on an exact tie choose the smaller cap.

edge_inputs.description: large values requiring a precise cap
tie must resolve to the smaller value
answer not present in the array
target far above the full sum keeps cap at the max
all equal elements with target between two cap sums

stress.description: large array with random values and target to exercise the search and tie rule
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 10000))
    arr = [rng.randint(1, 100000) for _ in range(n)]
    target = rng.randint(1, 100000)
    return [arr, target]
stress.note: values and target within stated bounds
